GenoSim 1.0.0

    • Introduction to GenoSim: Forward-Time Genotype Simulation

Simulate a population with stratification using Balding-Nichols model

Source: R/population_stratification.R
simulate_population_stratified.Rd

Generates a genotype matrix for a single population where allele frequencies are drawn from a Beta distribution parameterised by ancestral frequencies and population-specific Fst. This implements the Balding-Nichols model.

simulate_population_stratified(
  n_individuals,
  n_snps = NULL,
  population,
  ancestral_af = NULL,
  Fst = NULL,
  inbreeding_F = 0,
  seed = NULL,
  verbose = TRUE
)

Arguments

n_individuals

Integer. Number of diploid individuals to simulate.

n_snps

Integer. Number of SNPs (if `ancestral_af` is not provided).

population

Character. Population name (must be one of those returned by reference_populations).

ancestral_af

Either a numeric vector of length `n_snps` or a file path (passed to load_af_from_file). If `NULL`, uniform random frequencies are used as ancestral (not recommended).

Fst

Override the population-specific Fst. If `NULL`, uses the value from reference_populations.

inbreeding_F

Numeric in [0,1]. Inbreeding coefficient for all individuals (applied via .sample_genotypes_vec). Default 0.

seed

Random seed (optional).

verbose

Logical. Print progress.

Value

A genotype matrix (individuals x SNPs) with dosages 0,1,2.

Details

The Balding-Nichols model draws population-specific allele frequency \(p_{pop}\) for each SNP as: $$p_{pop} \sim \text{Beta}(\alpha, \beta)$$ where \(\alpha = p_{anc} \cdot (1-F_{st}) / F_{st}\) and \(\beta = (1-p_{anc}) \cdot (1-F_{st}) / F_{st}\). Then genotypes are sampled under Hardy‑Weinberg equilibrium (or with inbreeding if `inbreeding_F > 0`).

Examples

if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
# Simulate 100 individuals from South Asian population using gnomAD ancestral AFs
anc <- load_af_from_file("gnomad_global_af.csv")
geno <- simulate_population_stratified(
  n_individuals = 100,
  ancestral_af = anc,
  population = "SAS",
  seed = 123
)
} # }

Contents
